item.title: The Prospect of a Quieter Fed Could Roil Bond Prices
item.description: Warsh has hinted at less communication from THE central bank.

item.title: Corporate AI Has a Human Intelligence Mining Challenge
item.description: Much of the information about how work actually gets done lives in employees’ heads, and capturing this knowledge is hard.

item.title: Glencore, Trafigura Said Set to Invest in Merdeka’s HK Listing
item.description: Glencore Plc, Mercuria Energy Group and Trafigura Group are among key investors planning to buy shares in the Hong Kong listing of Indonesian gold miner PT Merdeka Gold Resources, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Don’t Get Overexcited on Oil Price Moderation, AIB’s Hunt Says
item.description: The global economy still faces multiple risks even as the US and Iran agree to halt the war in the Middle East, the head of Irish bank AIB Group Plc warned.

item.title: Europe Offers Cheaper AI Bets as Focus Shifts, Citi’s Badoy Says
item.description: European markets present cheaper opportunities in artificial intelligence adoption as the focus shifts from infrastructure to implementation, according to Elise Badoy of Citigroup Inc.

item.title: Turkey Seeks Higher Oil Flows Via Iraq Pipeline to Renew Deal
item.description: Turkey is seeking higher oil flows through a crucial pipeline that runs through its territory from northern Iraq in order to renew a contract that expires in a little over a month, according to a Turkish official with direct knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Riksbank Rate Seen Steady With Norway on Edge: Decision Guide
item.description: Sweden and Norway face contrasting economic risks, but the two Nordic neighbors are still likely to emerge with the same outcome of no change for borrowing costs this week.

item.title: Warsh Wants Less Fed Talk, Risking More Market Surprises
item.description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Ben Bernanke famously said monetary policy is 98% talk and only 2% action. Kevin Warsh wants to shake up the mix.

item.title: Blackstone Grows Asset-Based Lending With New Unit, Taps Garlick
item.description: Blackstone Inc. rolled out a platform to lend against physical assets like inventory and equipment, as alternative asset managers increasingly muscle into territory long dominated by banks.

item.title: Reeves Says Her Plans Will Make UK Less Reliant on Bond Markets
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said her ambition is to balance the budget in 2029, a more demanding target than her current fiscal rules as she pledged to make Britain less at the mercy of the bond markets.

item.title: Taiwan Frees Life Insurers to Deploy Capital in AI Projects
item.description: Taiwan is easing regulations to give life insurers flexibility to invest in artificial intelligence projects, part of an ongoing campaign by authorities to redirect a larger slice of the industry’s $1 trillion asset pool back home.

item.title: Emerging Stocks Near Record High on Tech Gains, Lower Oil Prices
item.description: Emerging-market stocks climbed for a third day, holding near a record high, as crude prices fell further and the tech rally extended.

item.title: AI Stock Rally Is in Boom Phase Not Euphoria, BofA Survey Shows
item.description: The boom in artificial-intelligence stocks looks set to run further, according to a Bank of America Corp. survey, which found the majority of investors believe that the fear of missing out is continuing to drive the trade.

item.title: German Investor Outlook Jumps With Iran Resolution in Sight
item.description: German investor optimism improved more than anticipated on hopes that a swift end to the Iran war will ease pressure on Europe’s largest economy.

item.title: Canada Home Sales Rise a Second Straight Month as Buyers Return
item.description: Home sales in Canada rose for a second consecutive month as depressed prices and a stabilizing economy translated into some tentative signs of momentum in the market.

item.title: China’s Xi Pledges Support for Myanmar During Leader’s Visit
item.description: China’s President Xi Jinping pledged support for neighboring Myanmar during a visit to Beijing by his counterpart Min Aung Hlaing.

item.title: Bank of Korea’s Minutes Show Wider Support for Hawkish Shift
item.description: South Korea’s central bank was already leaning toward higher rates at its May meeting, even with some policymakers who voted to keep borrowing costs unchanged arguing that inflation risks were starting to outweigh the costs of tighter policy.

item.title: French Security Service to Replace Palantir With Local Firm
item.description: France’s domestic intelligence agency, DGSI, will replace Palantir Technologies Inc. data tools with a local alternative made by Chapsvision, as European countries increasingly seek to reduce their dependence on US tech firms.

item.title: China’s Strong Bond Sale Pressures Yuan Liquidity in Hong Kong
item.description: Robust appetite for China’s offshore yuan bonds is aggravating a quarter-end liquidity squeeze in Hong Kong, sending funding costs to a two-month high amid a seasonal surge in cash demand.

item.title: SpaceX Stock Set for More Than 50% Jump in Just Three Sessions
item.description: SpaceX shares surged in premarket trading, putting the stock on track to extend a rally following its blockbuster debut last week.

item.title: India’s Richest Civic Body Delays Banker Bid Deadline for Bonds
item.description: India’s richest civic authority has extended the deadline for submitting bids on its 95 billion rupees ($1 billion) municipal offering, which is expected to be raised in one or more tranches.

item.title: Dubai Property Frenzy Cools But Sellers Hold the Line on Prices
item.description: Three months into a conflict that has roiled the Middle East, a nuanced picture is emerging on the state of Dubai’s housing market. The total value of transactions is down steeply, though prices have proved more resilient and buyers continue to seek out pockets of opportunity.

item.title: STMicro Eyes $1.5 Billion Convertible Bonds After AI-Fueled Jump
item.description: STMicroelectronics NV is looking to raise $1.5 billion from selling debt that can be exchanged into equity after the chipmaker’s shares tripled in value so far this year.

item.title: Israeli Bank to Sever West Bank Ties, Raising Alarm Over Economy
item.description: One of two Israeli banks giving Palestinians access to financial services plans to end the relationship in September, a move Palestinian officials warn could put further pressure on an economy already in turmoil.

item.title: Philippines Returns to Global Bond Market as Oil Pressure Eases
item.description: The Philippines is tapping the international bond market for the second time this year, seizing the opportunity to fund state spending as borrowing costs eased on optimism over a potential US-Iran agreement.

item.title: Equity Market Still Has a Long To-Do List After Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Pricing out a Middle East war looks like the easy part for equities, which must still confront a potentially hawkish new Federal Reserve chair, Washington’s disruptive intervention in the AI trade, and the biggest wave of stock supply in market history.

item.title: Thames Water Edges Closer to Nationalization With Rescue in Doubt
item.description: Thames Water edged nearer to temporary nationalization after a senior government minister said a rescue deal put forward by creditors wasn’t good enough.

item.title: Qatar Plans to Rapidly Restart LNG Production After Hormuz Opens
item.description: Qatar is planning to rapidly boost liquefied natural gas production once the Strait of Hormuz reopens, aiming to restore most of its export capacity within two months, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Sigma’s Doomed Boots Bid Highlights Australian Outbound M&A Woes
item.description: Australia has long been coveted by investment bankers in the Asia Pacific as a market where big mergers and acquisitions transactions take place, and therefore also where advisers make more money on fees.

item.title: Rathbones Shares Plunge on £60 Million Hit From FCA Review
item.description: Rathbones Group Plc shares plunged after the UK wealth manager said it expects to incur £60 million ($80.4 million) of additional costs over the next two years, as it addresses issues found in a watchdog review into how it treats its customers.
